articles.title: A “ghost” great white shark just reignited a 160-year Mediterranean mystery
articles.summary: The capture of a juvenile great white shark in Spain has provided fresh evidence that the Mediterranean's elusive "ghost" population of great whites still survives. Researchers reviewing 160 years of records say the discovery could even hint that the sharks are still breeding in the region.

articles.title: From pet to pest, goldfish can wreck entire ecosystems
articles.summary: A new study reveals that goldfish can do far more than survive in the wild—they can fundamentally reshape freshwater ecosystems. Researchers found they cloud water, damage food webs, and hurt native fish populations, sometimes triggering major ecological shifts.
